proposed_definition: A basal cell of the epidermis that is in an early developmental state, present in embryonic skin from approximately 7 post-conception weeks in humans. This cell expresses basal keratins (KRT5, KRT14) but lacks the molecular markers of mature basal keratinocytes (e.g., DPYSL2 expression) and is associated with early mesenchymal-epithelial signalling during hair follicle initiation. It decreases in abundance after 11 post-conception weeks during the transition from embryonic to fetal skin.
distinguishing_features: Immature developmental state, presence in embryonic (7-11 PCW) but not fetal skin, lacks DPYSL2 and POSTN expression that define mature basal subtypes
